description: TCM compounds with classical gout indication, triaged via the comp-004 IC50 occupancy + comp-007 composite scoring frameworks. Bioactive compound identification per tcm-modern-rigor-intersection.md candidate table. Bioavailability and dose values grep-verified against primary sources where possible; bounded placeholders elsewhere with explicit annotation.

compounds.name: Astilbin
compounds.tcm_source: Smilax glabra (土茯苓 / Tu Fu Ling)
compounds.chembl_search_result: No ChEMBL hit for 'astilbin' name search; no SMILES match at 85% similarity. Not curated in ChEMBL v34 as of 2026-05-06.
compounds.dose_source: [UNVERIFIED — bound: extrapolated from murine 5-20 mg/kg active dose (PMC9635963 ref 54, Wang 2016 PMID 27522260) × 60 kg adult ÷ 12 species scaling factor ≈ 25-100 mg human-equivalent; supplement dosing typically 100-500 mg crude extract]
compounds.bioavailability_source: [UNVERIFIED — bound: 1-10%. Astilbin glycosides are largely deglycosylated by gut microbiota; aglycone (taxifolin) bioavailability is ~6% per Lin 2013 PMID 23959242. Conservative 5% used.]
compounds.solubility_note: [UNVERIFIED — bound: glycoside form is moderately water-soluble; intestinal solubility likely 100-500 mg/L]
compounds.mechanism_note: Astilbin reduces hyperuricemia in mouse model via URAT1 expression downregulation (renal) per PMC9635963 §flavanonol; XO inhibition demonstrated in vitro per PMID 34885887. Systemic via aglycone (taxifolin) post-microbiome conversion; gut-luminal effect plausible.

compounds.name: Rhein
compounds.dose_source: Diacerein (rhein diacetate, CHEMBL41286 max_phase=3) FDA-approved dose 50-100 mg twice daily for osteoarthritis. Rhein per se rarely supplemented as pure compound.
compounds.bioavailability_source: Diacerein is a prodrug converted to rhein in vivo; rhein oral BA ~50% as carboxylate-anion. Inferred from diacerein PK literature.
compounds.solubility_note: [UNVERIFIED — bound: rhein has carboxylic acid; pH 7 solubility ~100-500 mg/L]
compounds.mechanism_note: Rhein claimed URAT1 inhibitor in TCM literature (mechanism extrapolation from in vivo rat data). NO ChEMBL bioactivity vs URAT1, ABCG2, XO, or NLRP3 (verified empty). Most-potent ChEMBL bioactivity: AChE IC50=637 nM (Electrophorus electricus), HSP90 IC50=3,339 nM, FTO IC50=2,180 nM. Off-target enriched.

compounds.name: Emodin
compounds.tcm_source: Rheum officinale (大黄 / Da Huang); also Polygonum cuspidatum (虎杖)
compounds.dose_source: Murine effective dose 30-50 mg/kg per PMC10304951 (Hou et al. 2023, doi:10.3390/ph16060789). Human-equivalent ~5-8 mg/kg via species scaling = 300-500 mg/60-kg adult; typical TCM-derived emodin supplement 25-100 mg.
compounds.bioavailability_source: [UNVERIFIED — bound: emodin systemic BA <5%; heavy gut/hepatic glucuronidation. Liu 2012 PMID 22968180 reports emodin Cmax ~0.05-0.5 μM after 50 mg/kg PO in rats — consistent with <5% BA]
compounds.solubility_note: Emodin is anthraquinone — very poor aqueous solubility ~0.5 mg/L. Intestinal solubility with bile salts ~5 mg/L (analogous to curcumin).
compounds.mechanism_note: PMC10304951 (Hou 2023): Emodin 30/50 mg/kg in PO rat model significantly lowered SUA via increased FEUA. Mechanism is NOT XO inhibition (XO activity unchanged in study). Likely renal urate transporter modulation. NO ChEMBL bioactivity vs URAT1/ABCG2/XO/NLRP3. Most-potent ChEMBL: CK2α IC50=890 nM, ER-α IC50=2,700 nM.

compounds.name: Aucubin
compounds.tcm_source: Plantago asiatica (车前子 / Che Qian Zi)
compounds.chembl_search_result: No ChEMBL hit for 'aucubin' name search. Not curated in ChEMBL v34 as of 2026-05-06.
compounds.dose_source: [UNVERIFIED — placeholder: extrapolated from Plantago asiatica seed (Che Qian Zi) 9-15 g/day in TCM decoctions; aucubin content varies 1-5%; estimate 100-500 mg aucubin daily]
compounds.bioavailability_source: [UNVERIFIED — bound: iridoid glycoside; gut microbiota deglycosylation; aglycone bioavailability low; ~1-10% systemic. Conservative 5% used.]
compounds.solubility_note: Aucubin is a polar glycoside — highly water-soluble (>1 g/L).
compounds.mechanism_note: Aucubin/Plantago asiatica claimed uricosuric in classical TCM. NO ChEMBL bioactivity for aucubin against any target. Mechanism extrapolation only — diuretic effect plausible via aquaporin or RAS modulation but not gout-specific.

compounds.name: Berberine
compounds.tcm_source: Coptis chinensis (黄连 / Huang Lian); also Phellodendron amurense (黄柏 / Huang Bai)
compounds.dose_source: Standard berberine HCl supplement dosing 500-1500 mg/day, typically 500 mg TID. Confirmed in commercial supplement PI sheets.
compounds.bioavailability_source: Berberine oral systemic BA <1%. Confirmed ChEMBL_supplements_stack.md citation; PMC8665891 (Petrangolini 2021, doi:10.1155/2021/7563889) explicitly developed enhanced formulations for absorption. Standard form ~0.5%.
compounds.solubility_note: Berberine HCl is moderately water-soluble (~200 mg/L) but absorption is limited by P-gp efflux and self-aggregation, not solubility.
compounds.mechanism_note: Berberine 6.25-25 mg/kg in mouse PO model inhibits URAT1 expression, promotes urate excretion (PMC9635963 ref 70-71). Most-potent ChEMBL bioactivity: TDO IC50=30 nM (verified), CYP1B1 IC50=94 nM, AChE IC50=100 nM, IDO1 IC50=260 nM. NO direct URAT1/ABCG2/XO IC50 in ChEMBL. NLRP3 effect via NF-κB modulation (indirect).

compounds.name: Cylindrin
compounds.tcm_source: Imperata cylindrica (白茅根 / Bai Mao Gen)
compounds.chembl_search_result: No ChEMBL hit for 'cylindrin' or 'arundoin' name search.
compounds.dose_source: [UNVERIFIED — placeholder: Imperata cylindrica root (Bai Mao Gen) is used 9-30 g/day in TCM decoctions; cylindrin content very low ~0.1%; estimate <50 mg per dose]
compounds.bioavailability_source: [UNVERIFIED — bound: triterpenes are highly lipophilic, low aqueous BA, ~1-5%]
compounds.solubility_note: [UNVERIFIED — bound: triterpene; aqueous solubility likely <1 mg/L]
compounds.mechanism_note: Imperata cylindrica claimed diuretic/uricosuric in classical TCM. NO ChEMBL bioactivity. Mechanism unknown — likely diuretic via aquaporin or sodium handling, not gout-specific.

compounds.name: Chlorogenic acid
compounds.tcm_source: Lonicera japonica (金银花 / Jin Yin Hua); also coffee, many plants
compounds.dose_source: Green coffee extract supplements typically standardized to 50% chlorogenic acids; doses 200-1000 mg/day.
compounds.bioavailability_source: [UNVERIFIED — bound: chlorogenic acid systemic BA varies 8-33% across studies; ~33% from healthy ileostomy subjects per Stalmach 2010 PMID 19735245. Significant gut microbiota metabolism to caffeic acid + quinic acid.]
compounds.solubility_note: Highly water-soluble (>5 g/L). Solubility not limiting.
compounds.mechanism_note: ChEMBL most-potent: PTP1B IC50=100 nM, AKR1B1 IC50=300 nM, ONOO scavenging IC50=520 nM. NO direct URAT1/ABCG2/XO IC50. Mechanism for gout via anti-inflammatory and antioxidant pathways — Mechanistic Extrapolation.

compounds.name: Luteolin
compounds.tcm_source: Lonicera japonica (金银花 / Jin Yin Hua); also Achyranthes bidentata (component of Si Miao San)
compounds.bioavailability_source: [UNVERIFIED — bound: Luteolin systemic BA ~5% (heavy glucuronidation). Shimoi 1998 PMID 9625363 reports plasma luteolin glucuronide as the main circulating form.]
compounds.solubility_note: Luteolin aqueous solubility low (~5 mg/L); intestinal with bile salts ~50 mg/L.
compounds.mechanism_note: ChEMBL XO IC50=550 nM (best, J Nat Prod 1998), 7,800 nM (Bioorg Med Chem Lett 2015), Ki=2,900 nM (Eur J Med Chem 2014). ABCG2 IC50=8,900 nM (J Nat Prod 2011). CYP1B1 IC50=79 nM. URAT1 expression downregulation in mouse PO model 3-10 mg/kg per PMC9635963 ref 45 (Lin 2018 PMID 29519319).

compounds.name: Atractylenolide_I
compounds.tcm_source: Atractylodes macrocephala / lancea (苍术 / Cang Zhu — Si Miao San component)
compounds.chembl_search_result: No ChEMBL hit for 'atractylenolide I' or 'eudesmol' name search.
compounds.dose_source: [UNVERIFIED — placeholder: Atractylodes rhizome (Cang Zhu) used 6-12 g/day in TCM; atractylenolide I content ~0.1-0.5%; estimate 10-50 mg per dose]
compounds.bioavailability_source: [UNVERIFIED — bound: sesquiterpene lactone; lipophilic; estimated 5-20% BA]
compounds.solubility_note: [UNVERIFIED — bound: sesquiterpene lactone; lipophilic; aqueous solubility ~1 mg/L]
compounds.mechanism_note: Component of Si Miao San (Cang Zhu) — anti-inflammatory in TCM gout formulas. NO ChEMBL bioactivity. HDAC inhibition claim from Brian's brief is speculative; not in ChEMBL or comp-007 corpus. Si Miao San efficacy demonstrated as formula (PMC5360963 meta-analysis: −90.62 µmol/L SUA reduction) but cannot attribute to single component.

compounds_searched_no_chembl: aloe-emodin — no hit (parent search returned only diacerein)
verification_protocol: ChEMBL queries via mcp__plugin_chembl_ChEMBL__compound_search and get_bioactivity. PubMed via mcp__plugin_pubmed_PubMed__search_articles + get_article_metadata (DOIs cited). Paperclip grep for primary-source verification per pre-commit verification gate.
